title: Open problem: convex drawings vs. pseudocircular drawings
statement_text: The relation between convex drawings and pseudocircular drawings remains open.
context_text: Arroyo, Richter, and Sunohara proved that hereditary-convex drawings are precisely the pseudospherical drawings (i.e., pseudocircular drawings satisfying two additional properties). The analogous characterisation for (non-hereditary) convex drawings in terms of pseudocircular drawings has not been established.
notes: Stated in running prose without a labelled environment, in Section 2.1. The dedicated open-problems section (Section 7, explicitly announced in the introduction) is not present in the provided PDF extraction, so further conjectures and questions from that section could not be captured.
paper_title: Topological Drawings meet Classical Theorems from Convex Geometry
